project.name: lightrag-hku
project.description: LightRAG is a graph-based Retrieval-Augmented Generation framework that extracts entities and relationships from documents to construct a knowledge graph, then uses multiple retrieval modes (local, global, hybrid, naive, mix) to augment LLM responses with structured domain knowledge. It supports 12+ storage backends, a FastAPI REST server with WebUI, and an Ollama-compatible API.

nodes.name: Document Ingestion
nodes.summary: Handles the full lifecycle of ingesting source documents into LightRAG: accepting uploads or text insertions, routing to parser engines (DOCX, Docling, MinerU, plain text), splitting parsed content into token-bounded chunks, embedding chunks into the vector store, and tracking per-document processing status across pipeline concurrency slots.

businessRules: Documents must pass path sanitization before parsing
Pipeline queue size limits concurrency via slot reservation
Chunking configuration merges document-level and global settings
Oversized chunks are recursively split to fit embedding model token limits
Failed documents update status to failed and release pipeline resources
crossDomainInteractions: Parsed chunks are forwarded to Knowledge Graph Construction for entity/relation extraction
Pipeline status and chunk vectors are persisted via Storage Management
Ingestion is triggered by API & Integration via REST document upload endpoints

nodes.name: Knowledge Graph Construction
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the LLM-driven extraction of entities and relationships from text chunks, merges extracted data into a persistent knowledge graph with description reconciliation and edge-weight accumulation, and supports manual graph editing (create, update, delete, merge) via API. Manages extraction prompt profiles, deduplication, and concurrent upsert coordination.

businessRules: Entity extraction uses YAML-configurable prompt profiles with entity type lists
Entity descriptions exceeding length thresholds are compressed via LLM summarization
Edge weights accumulate across all document chunks for temporal relationship strength
Concurrent upserts use per-entity distributed locks to prevent race conditions
Storage dirty flags coordinate multi-worker graph state propagation
crossDomainInteractions: Consumes text chunks from Document Ingestion pipeline
Writes entities, relationships, and summaries to Storage Management backends
Graph state is read by Query & Retrieval during knowledge graph traversal

nodes.name: Query & Retrieval
nodes.summary: Implements five retrieval modes — local (entity neighborhood), global (community-level), hybrid (combined), naive (chunk vector only), and mix (graph + naive) — to answer user queries by combining knowledge graph traversal with vector similarity search and LLM response synthesis. Supports streaming responses via Server-Sent Events and conversation history management.

businessRules: Retrieval mode (local/global/hybrid/naive/mix) determines graph traversal strategy
Context window is assembled within a configurable token budget
LLM keyword extraction drives entity vector search for graph-based modes
Conversation history is maintained per-session for multi-turn queries
Streaming mode delivers tokens progressively via SSE without response buffering
crossDomainInteractions: Traverses the knowledge graph built by Knowledge Graph Construction
Reads from vector and graph storage backends managed by Storage Management
Query endpoints are exposed to clients via API & Integration

nodes.name: Storage Management
nodes.summary: Provides a pluggable multi-backend storage layer for KV caches, vector databases, graph stores, and document-status tracking. Handles backend instantiation via a class factory, schema migrations at startup, LLM response caching with eviction, and offline migration tools for moving cache data between backends. Supports 12+ backends including Neo4j, PostgreSQL, Qdrant, Redis, Chroma, MongoDB, and more.

businessRules: Storage backends are selected by name from a registry and instantiated via factory pattern
All backends must implement the abstract interface before accepting traffic
Schema migrations run automatically at startup for schema-managed backends
LLM response caches are keyed by prompt hash and evicted on knowledge graph updates
Namespace isolation enables multi-tenant and multi-workspace deployments
crossDomainInteractions: Serves persistent storage to Document Ingestion, Knowledge Graph Construction, and Query & Retrieval
Provides the factory and abstract base classes used by all storage-consuming domains
Cache migration tools move data between backends offline

nodes.name: API & Integration
nodes.summary: Exposes LightRAG capabilities over a FastAPI REST server with JWT/API-key authentication, covering document management, query execution, knowledge graph CRUD, and system health. Includes an Ollama-compatible API layer for clients expecting OpenAI-compatible or Ollama chat interfaces. Manages server startup, CORS, root-path middleware, and Uvicorn/Gunicorn process lifecycle.

businessRules: All protected routes require a valid JWT bearer token or API key
Ollama model name field encodes LightRAG query mode and parameters
Server configuration is assembled from CLI args and environment variables at startup
Multiple Uvicorn workers share storage state via cross-process synchronization flags
Auth is skippable in development via LIGHTRAG_API_KEY=none
crossDomainInteractions: Triggers document ingestion pipeline via Document Ingestion REST routes
Dispatches queries to Query & Retrieval engine
Mutates knowledge graph via Knowledge Graph Construction CRUD routes
Served through WebUI Interaction frontend SPA

nodes.name: WebUI Interaction
nodes.summary: Provides an interactive React single-page application for exploring, editing, and querying the LightRAG knowledge base. Features a sigma.js-based knowledge graph visualization canvas, a streaming Markdown retrieval testing interface, and document management screens for uploading, monitoring, and deleting documents. All data flows through the FastAPI REST backend.

businessRules: JWT tokens are refreshed automatically on 401 responses from the backend
Graph layout is computed client-side using graphology force-directed algorithms
Streaming query responses are consumed via EventSource / fetch with SSE parsing
Document deletions require confirmation and optionally remove source files
WebUI is served from the FastAPI server as a static SPA under /webui
crossDomainInteractions: All WebUI operations call REST endpoints provided by API & Integration
Knowledge graph data rendered by WebUI originates from Knowledge Graph Construction

nodes.name: File Upload & Parsing
nodes.summary: Accepts document uploads (files or raw text) through REST endpoints, validates and sanitizes the input, routes to the appropriate parser engine based on file type, and extracts structured text blocks, tables, and images from the document content.

nodes.name: Text Chunking & Embedding
nodes.summary: Splits parsed document text into token-bounded overlapping chunks using a configurable strategy, builds normalized chunk dictionaries with content-hashed IDs, enforces embedding model token limits, and stores chunk vectors in the vector database.

domainMeta.entryPoint: Internal: called after file parsing completes in lightrag.ainsert()

nodes.name: Pipeline Lifecycle Management
nodes.summary: Manages the concurrency slot reservation, document processing status transitions, error handling, and multi-worker coordination across the document ingestion pipeline. Ensures pipeline health information is available to the REST API and WebUI.

nodes.name: Entity & Relationship Extraction
nodes.summary: Uses a configurable LLM to extract named entities and typed relationships from each text chunk, parses the tuple-delimited output, generates summaries for verbose descriptions, and validates the extracted data before graph merge.

domainMeta.entryPoint: Internal: extract_entities() in lightrag/operate.py triggered per chunk batch

nodes.name: Knowledge Graph Merge
nodes.summary: Reconciles newly extracted entities and relationships with existing graph state by merging descriptions, accumulating edge weights, and performing concurrent upserts to graph storage and KV caches under distributed locks.

nodes.name: Graph Entity CRUD
nodes.summary: Handles manual creation, update, deletion, and merge of graph entities and relationships via REST API calls. Validates requests, applies changes to graph storage, synchronizes KV caches, and propagates storage update flags.

nodes.name: Graph-Based RAG Query
nodes.summary: Processes user queries in local, global, hybrid, or mix retrieval modes by extracting keywords with an LLM, searching entity vectors, traversing the knowledge graph to build a contextual window, and synthesizing a grounded response with the query LLM.

nodes.name: Naive Vector Query
nodes.summary: Performs direct vector similarity search over the chunk vector store without knowledge graph traversal, assembles the top-k most relevant chunks into a context window, and generates a response using the query LLM.

nodes.name: Streaming Response Delivery
nodes.summary: Wraps query execution in an SSE streaming response, emitting LLM response tokens progressively to the client as Server-Sent Events. Handles streaming errors and cleans up stream resources on completion.

nodes.name: Storage Backend Initialization
nodes.summary: Resolves configured backend names, instantiates KV, vector, graph, and doc-status storage classes through the class factory, runs startup schema migrations, validates interface compliance, and initializes shared pipeline state for multi-worker deployments.

domainMeta.entryPoint: Internal: LightRAG.__init__() → initialize() on server startup

nodes.name: LLM Cache Management
nodes.summary: Intercepts LLM completion and embedding calls to serve responses from KV cache on cache hits, persist new responses on misses, and evict query-mode caches when the knowledge graph is updated to ensure result freshness.

domainMeta.entryPoint: Internal: call_llm_func_with_cache() / compute_args_hash() on every LLM call

nodes.name: Cache Migration Between Backends
nodes.summary: Offline tool flow to migrate LLM extraction and summary caches from one storage backend to another, paginating through source records in configurable batches and reporting migration statistics.

nodes.name: Server Startup & Configuration
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full LightRAG API server boot sequence: parsing all CLI/environment configuration, validating provider bindings, instantiating the LightRAG engine with storage backends and LLM roles, registering all FastAPI routers, and launching the ASGI server.

nodes.name: User Authentication
nodes.summary: Handles credential validation via username/password or API key, issues signed JWT access tokens, and enforces authentication on all protected routes via a combined FastAPI dependency that validates bearer tokens and API keys.

nodes.name: Ollama-Compatible API
nodes.summary: Provides an Ollama-protocol-compatible API layer at /api/chat and /api/generate, translating the Ollama model name field to LightRAG query mode settings, delegating to the core query engine, and streaming back responses in Ollama JSON chunk format.

nodes.name: Knowledge Graph Visualization
nodes.summary: Fetches graph entity and relationship data from the REST API, computes a force-directed layout, renders an interactive sigma.js WebGL canvas with entity-type color coding, and supports node inspection and in-place entity property editing.

nodes.name: Retrieval Testing
nodes.summary: Provides an interactive query playground for configuring LightRAG retrieval settings, submitting streaming queries to the backend, rendering streamed Markdown responses with syntax highlighting, and displaying retrieved context references.

nodes.name: Document Management via WebUI
nodes.summary: Lets users upload document files via drag-and-drop with per-file progress indicators, monitor the ingestion pipeline status in real time, browse the paginated document index with processing states, and delete individual or all documents with confirmation.

nodes.name: Receive Document
nodes.summary: Accept document upload request via multipart file upload or raw text insertion, extracting file content, filename, metadata, and optional per-document chunking configuration from the request.

nodes.name: Validate & Sanitize Input
nodes.summary: Sanitize uploaded filenames to prevent path traversal, validate file paths against the configured input directory, check for duplicate documents, and enforce concurrency limits by verifying pipeline slot availability.

nodes.name: Route to Parser Engine
nodes.summary: Resolve the appropriate parser engine (native DOCX, Docling external tool, MinerU external tool, or plain text fallback) for the file type based on global parser routing config and filename extension hints.

nodes.name: Extract Structured Content
nodes.summary: Execute the resolved parser to extract structured text blocks, tables, equations, and embedded images from the document, producing an intermediate artifact with typed block sequences.

nodes.name: Store Parsed Artifacts
nodes.summary: Persist parsed artifacts (text, .blocks.jsonl structured output, extracted images) to the configured working directory and update the document processing status to reflect successful parsing.

nodes.name: Select Chunking Strategy
nodes.summary: Resolve the effective chunking strategy (token-size, recursive-character, paragraph-semantic, or semantic-vector) by merging document-level override configuration with the global AddonParams chunking settings.

nodes.name: Split Text into Chunks
nodes.summary: Split document text into token-bounded overlapping chunks using the selected strategy, respecting heading boundaries, overlap window size, and table structure integrity to preserve semantic coherence.

nodes.name: Build Chunk Dictionary
nodes.summary: Construct normalized chunk dictionaries with stable content-hashed IDs, chunk index, full document content back-reference, and source document metadata for downstream embedding and graph extraction.

nodes.name: Enforce Token Limits
nodes.summary: Detect chunks that exceed the embedding model's maximum token limit and recursively split them by word count to ensure all chunks fit within model context before batch embedding.

nodes.name: Embed & Store Chunks
nodes.summary: Submit chunk batches to the configured embedding function with request-level batching and content deduplication, then upsert the resulting vectors and metadata into the chunk vector database namespace.

nodes.name: Reserve Pipeline Slot
nodes.summary: Atomically reserve a concurrency slot in the document processing pipeline queue, returning HTTP 503 when the pipeline is full or locked, to prevent resource exhaustion under concurrent upload load.

nodes.name: Track Processing Status
nodes.summary: Persist document processing status transitions (pending → processing → processed/failed) to the DocStatusStorage backend, enabling the REST API and WebUI to report real-time pipeline progress.

nodes.name: Handle Pipeline Errors
nodes.summary: Catch extraction or storage exceptions during document processing, update the document status to failed with structured error detail, and release the pipeline slot to unblock subsequent uploads.

nodes.name: Finalize & Notify
nodes.summary: Mark document processing as complete by writing the final processed status, releasing the pipeline slot, and propagating shared storage update flags to coordinate multi-worker Gunicorn instances.

nodes.name: Load Extraction Prompt Profile
nodes.summary: Load the entity extraction prompt profile from a YAML configuration file or built-in defaults, resolving the entity type list, few-shot examples, and language-specific instruction templates.

nodes.name: Invoke LLM for Extraction
nodes.summary: Invoke the configured extraction LLM with prompt, chunk content, and few-shot examples, using the priority async work queue for concurrency control, with LLM response caching and automatic retries on failure.

nodes.name: Parse & Normalize LLM Output
nodes.summary: Parse the LLM's structured tuple-delimited extraction output, normalize entity type casing, sanitize delimiter-corrupted strings, deduplicate entity names within the batch, and handle continuation responses for long outputs.

nodes.name: Generate Entity Summaries
nodes.summary: Use the summary LLM role to generate compressed descriptive summaries for entities and relationships whose combined descriptions exceed the configured length threshold, preserving semantics while fitting storage limits.

nodes.name: Validate Extracted Data
nodes.summary: Validate extracted entity and relationship records against schema constraints — checking required fields, type conformance, and source chunk references — and filter out malformed records before passing to graph merge.

nodes.name: Check Existing Nodes
nodes.summary: Query the entity and relationship KV caches to find existing node and edge records matching the extracted entity names and relationship source-target pairs before applying merge logic.

nodes.name: Merge Node Descriptions
nodes.summary: Reconcile extracted entity descriptions with existing records by appending new source descriptions, merging source chunk ID sets, and triggering LLM summarization when the combined description length exceeds the threshold.

nodes.name: Reconcile Edge Weights
nodes.summary: Accumulate relationship occurrence weights from new extractions with existing edge weights, merge source chunk reference lists, and merge relationship keyword sets to strengthen temporal relationship signals.

nodes.name: Upsert Nodes to Graph
nodes.summary: Perform concurrent upserts of merged entity nodes to both the graph storage backend and entity KV cache, using per-entity asyncio locks to prevent race conditions across concurrent extraction workers.

nodes.name: Upsert Edges to Graph
nodes.summary: Perform concurrent edge upserts to graph storage and relationship KV cache, then set shared storage update flags to notify multi-worker deployments that the knowledge graph state has changed.

nodes.name: Validate Entity Request
nodes.summary: Validate incoming entity create, update, or merge request data against Pydantic request models, checking required fields, type constraints, and presence of the target entity/relationship identifiers.

nodes.name: Apply CRUD Operation
nodes.summary: Execute the graph mutation (create node, update properties, delete node or edge, or merge duplicate entities) against the graph storage backend, handling not-found errors with appropriate HTTP status codes.

nodes.name: Sync KV Caches
nodes.summary: Synchronize entity and relationship KV caches after structural graph changes by deleting or updating affected cache entries to maintain consistency between the cache layer and graph storage.

nodes.name: Propagate Update Flags
nodes.summary: Set shared storage update flags to notify co-located workers and the WebUI that knowledge graph state has changed, enabling query cache eviction and graph reloads.

nodes.name: Extract Query Keywords
nodes.summary: Use the keyword-extraction LLM role to extract high-level (global) and low-level (local) keywords from the user's query, driving entity vector search and graph traversal in graph-aware retrieval modes.

nodes.name: Search Entity Vectors
nodes.summary: Perform vector similarity search against the entity vector database using the extracted keyword embeddings to retrieve candidate entities and community reports matching the query intent.

nodes.name: Traverse Knowledge Graph
nodes.summary: Traverse the knowledge graph from matched entity seed nodes to retrieve connected nodes, edges, relationship descriptions, and multi-hop neighbor context according to the selected retrieval mode (local breadth-first, global community, hybrid combined, or mix).

nodes.name: Build Context Window
nodes.summary: Assemble the LLM context window from ranked entity descriptions, relationship summaries, and supporting text chunks, enforcing a configurable token budget and including conversation history for multi-turn interactions.

nodes.name: Synthesize LLM Response
nodes.summary: Call the query LLM with the assembled context window and system prompt to generate a grounded, citation-supported response, returning the result to the caller or streaming it via SSE.

nodes.name: Embed Query Text
nodes.summary: Generate a dense embedding for the user query using the configured embedding model, optionally injecting an asymmetric query prefix to distinguish query embeddings from document passage embeddings.

nodes.name: Search Chunk Vectors
nodes.summary: Execute cosine similarity search against the chunk vector database scoped to the working namespace to retrieve the top-k most semantically similar text chunks for the query.

nodes.name: Assemble Ranked Context
nodes.summary: Rank the retrieved chunks by similarity score, truncate to fit the configured context token budget, and concatenate chunk content into a formatted context string for LLM input.

nodes.name: Generate Naive Response
nodes.summary: Submit the assembled chunk context with the query and system prompt to the query LLM to generate a response based purely on retrieved text passages, without any knowledge graph enrichment.

nodes.name: Validate Streaming Request
nodes.summary: Authenticate the streaming query request, validate the QueryParam fields including mode and context limits, and confirm the query engine is initialized before opening the response stream.

nodes.name: Initiate SSE Stream
nodes.summary: Create a FastAPI StreamingResponse wrapping an async generator, setting SSE content-type headers and optionally including CORS headers to begin the server-sent event channel.

nodes.name: Emit Response Chunks
nodes.summary: Yield individual LLM response token batches as SSE data frames to the client, optionally including a final metadata frame with retrieved context sources and token usage statistics.

nodes.name: Handle Stream Termination
nodes.summary: Detect LLM or network errors during streaming, forward errors as SSE error events to the client for graceful degradation, and ensure the async generator finalizes cleanly on both success and failure paths.

nodes.name: Resolve Backend Names
nodes.summary: Read KV, vector, graph, and doc-status storage backend names from the LightRAG config object or environment variables and map them to concrete implementation class names via the backend registry.

nodes.name: Instantiate via Factory
nodes.summary: Dynamically import and instantiate each storage class with its namespace, workspace, connection URI, and LightRAG config reference, returning fully-configured backend objects ready for operation.

nodes.name: Run Schema Migrations
nodes.summary: Execute startup schema migrations and data backfills for schema-managed backends (PostgreSQL, Oracle, etc.) using the storage migration module to align the database schema with the current application version.

nodes.name: Validate Interface Compliance
nodes.summary: Verify that each instantiated storage backend correctly implements all abstract methods from its base class before the application accepts traffic, raising configuration errors for non-compliant backends.

nodes.name: Initialize Shared State
nodes.summary: Initialize cross-process pipeline status flags, document queue state, and namespace-keyed shared memory structures to coordinate state between multiple Gunicorn worker processes.

nodes.name: Lookup Cache Key
nodes.summary: Compute a deterministic hash key from the LLM function arguments (prompt, model name, temperature) and query the KV cache storage for an existing matching completion or embedding entry.

nodes.name: Return Cached Response
nodes.summary: Return the stored LLM completion or embedding directly from the KV cache on a cache hit, tracking a cache-hit event for token usage and cost monitoring without invoking the LLM provider.

nodes.name: Store New Response
nodes.summary: Persist the fresh LLM completion or embedding to the KV cache under the computed hash key for future cache hits, reducing repeated API calls for identical prompts.

nodes.name: Evict Stale Query Caches
nodes.summary: Selectively delete cached responses for query modes (local, global, hybrid, mix) from KV storage when the knowledge graph is updated, preventing stale responses from being returned after graph mutations.

nodes.name: Discover Cache Keys
nodes.summary: Enumerate all extraction-cache and summary-cache keys stored in the source storage backend using backend-specific key listing APIs, producing a paginated iterator over all migratable records.

nodes.name: Paginate Source Records
nodes.summary: Read cache records from the source backend in configurable batch sizes, tracking progress with counters and handling backend-specific read errors with per-record isolation to avoid failing the entire migration.

nodes.name: Write to Target Backend
nodes.summary: Batch-write migrated cache records to the target storage backend, handling individual write failures with error logging and retry logic without aborting the overall migration batch.

nodes.name: Report Migration Statistics
nodes.summary: Print a structured migration summary to stdout showing total records processed, counts of successfully migrated, failed, and skipped records per batch, and elapsed time statistics.

nodes.name: Parse CLI & Env Config
nodes.summary: Parse all server CLI arguments and environment variables into the global config singleton, covering LLM provider bindings, storage backend selection, authentication settings, CORS policy, and Uvicorn/Gunicorn worker configuration.

nodes.name: Validate Storage & LLM Bindings
nodes.summary: Verify storage backend compatibility, LLM and embedding provider function bindings, AWS Bedrock authentication, and asymmetric embedding prefix configuration before engine initialization.

nodes.name: Initialize LightRAG Engine
nodes.summary: Instantiate the LightRAG orchestrator with all configured storage backends, LLM role assignments (extraction, query, keyword, summarize), addon parameters, and working directory paths.

nodes.name: Register Routers & Middleware
nodes.summary: Wire FastAPI routers for documents, queries, graphs, Ollama compatibility, and health endpoints. Configure CORS settings, authentication dependency injection, root-path prefix middleware, and static SPA file serving.

nodes.name: Launch ASGI Server
nodes.summary: Start the ASGI server using Uvicorn for development single-worker mode or Gunicorn with the Uvicorn worker class for production multi-worker deployments, applying SSL, port, and host binding settings.

nodes.name: Receive Credentials
nodes.summary: Accept user credentials from the login endpoint (username and password in form body) or an API key via the Authorization header or X-API-Key header for machine-to-machine authentication.

nodes.name: Validate Password Backend
nodes.summary: Verify submitted credentials against the configured accounts store using bcrypt hash comparison for hashed passwords or plain-text equality for development configurations, rejecting unknown usernames.

nodes.name: Issue JWT Token
nodes.summary: Generate a signed JWT access token containing the username claim and configured expiry timestamp, returning it in the login response for subsequent use as a bearer token.

nodes.name: Enforce Auth Dependency
nodes.summary: Validate the bearer token or API key on every protected FastAPI route using the combined authentication dependency, decoding the JWT signature and expiry or matching the static API key, returning 401 on failure.

nodes.name: Receive Ollama Request
nodes.summary: Accept POST requests on /api/chat or /api/generate conforming to the Ollama protocol, parsing the model name, messages array, stream flag, and optional options block from the request body.

nodes.name: Parse Model Name to Query Mode
nodes.summary: Extract and decode the LightRAG query mode (local, global, hybrid, naive, mix) and optional QueryParam overrides from the Ollama model name field using a structured naming convention.

nodes.name: Delegate to LightRAG Query
nodes.summary: Forward the decoded query text and QueryParam configuration to the LightRAG engine's aquery() method, passing conversation history derived from the messages array.

nodes.name: Stream Ollama-Format Response
nodes.summary: Format LightRAG query response tokens as Ollama JSON chunk objects and stream them to the client, including a final done=true sentinel chunk with eval_duration and token count metadata.

nodes.name: Fetch Graph from API
nodes.summary: Fetch paginated entity nodes and relationship edge data from the REST API using the typed lightrag.ts API client, with configurable node and edge limits and workspace filters.

nodes.name: Run Graph Layout
nodes.summary: Build a graphology Graph instance from the fetched entity and relationship data, then compute node positions using a force-directed (FA2) or circular layout algorithm before canvas rendering.

nodes.name: Render sigma.js Canvas
nodes.summary: Mount a sigma.js WebGL renderer onto the graphology instance, applying entity-type color coding, edge thickness by weight, and zoom/pan interaction controls with node degree-based size scaling.

nodes.name: Select & Inspect Node
nodes.summary: Handle sigma node click and hover events to highlight the selected entity, display its description, type, source chunks, and connected relationship list in the properties sidebar panel.

nodes.name: Edit Entity Properties
nodes.summary: Open an inline edit dialog to modify entity name, description, or relationship properties, then persist changes to the backend via REST API PATCH/PUT calls and refresh the graph canvas state.

nodes.name: Configure Query Parameters
nodes.summary: Let users configure LightRAG QueryParam settings including retrieval mode, top-k entity limits, context token size, history window length, reranking flags, and custom system prompt through the QuerySettings panel.

nodes.name: Submit Streaming Query
nodes.summary: Submit the query as a streaming fetch request to the /query/stream endpoint, handling SSE chunk parsing, automatic JWT token refresh on 401 responses, and progress state management.

nodes.name: Render Streamed Markdown
nodes.summary: Display the streaming LLM response with real-time Markdown rendering, syntax-highlighted code blocks, inline math rendering, collapsible JSON blocks, and footnote citation links.

nodes.name: Display Query References
nodes.summary: Show retrieved context references, source document IDs, related entity links, and token usage statistics in the response panel after streaming completes.

nodes.name: Upload Documents
nodes.summary: Accept file selection via drag-and-drop or file picker and upload files individually to the REST document endpoint with per-file progress indicators and queue management.

nodes.name: Monitor Pipeline Status
nodes.summary: Poll the /documents/pipeline-status endpoint on a configurable interval to display real-time pipeline queue depth, processing state breakdown (pending/processing/processed/failed counts), and busy/idle indicator.

nodes.name: Browse Document Index
nodes.summary: Display a paginated, sortable table of all ingested documents with status badges (pending, processing, processed, failed), file size, and creation timestamps with multi-select support.

nodes.name: Delete or Clear Documents
nodes.summary: Delete selected documents or clear all documents via REST API with a typed confirmation dialog, optionally triggering source file removal from disk and LLM query cache eviction.

edges.description: Parsed and chunked document text is forwarded to the Knowledge Graph Construction domain where entity and relationship extraction is performed on each chunk batch.
Document processing status updates and embedded chunk vectors are persisted to DocStatusStorage and vector storage backends managed by the Storage Management domain.
Extracted and merged entity nodes, relationship edges, and description summaries are written to graph storage, entity vector DB, and KV caches managed by the Storage Management domain.
Graph-based RAG queries traverse entity nodes and relationship edges built by the Knowledge Graph Construction domain to assemble the retrieval context window.
All retrieval modes read from vector storage (entity and chunk vector DBs) and graph storage backends managed and initialized by the Storage Management domain.
REST document upload and text insertion endpoints in the API & Integration domain trigger the Document Ingestion pipeline flows.
The /query and /query/stream REST endpoints exposed by API & Integration dispatch query requests to the Query & Retrieval domain engine.
Graph CRUD REST routes in API & Integration delegate entity and relationship create/update/delete operations to the Knowledge Graph Construction domain.
Server startup initializes all storage backends via the Storage Management domain before registering API routes and accepting traffic.
All WebUI operations — graph data fetches, streaming queries, document uploads, entity edits — are proxied through the REST API endpoints provided by the API & Integration domain.
Storage Management provides the abstract graph and KV storage interfaces that Knowledge Graph Construction depends on for reading, writing, and locking entity and relationship data.
